definitions.articularis genu muscle: A skeletal muscle of the anterior thigh that arises from the anterior surface of the distal femoral shaft, deep to the vastus intermedius, and inserts into the synovial membrane of the knee joint; functions to retract the suprapatellar bursa and prevent synovial impingement during knee extension; innervated by the femoral nerve.

notes.fma_parent_resolution: FMA:22424 is 'Muscle of anterior compartment of thigh'. No equivalent UBERON term exists for this specific compartment class. The closest available parent is UBERON:0004252 'hindlimb stylopod muscle' (synonym: 'muscle of thigh'; xref: FMA:22470), which is the general thigh muscle grouping. UBERON:0002463 exists for posterior compartment (FMA:22427) but no analogous anterior compartment term is present in UBERON.
notes.existing_term_check: No existing UBERON term found for articularis genu muscle (searched: 'articularis genu', 'articularis genu muscle', 'subcrureus', 'articular muscle of knee'). This is a new term.
notes.parent_candidate_considered: UBERON:0004252 (hindlimb stylopod muscle) — general thigh muscle grouping, best available parent given absence of anterior compartment term. A more specific parent could be created (muscle of anterior compartment of hindlimb stylopod, analogous to UBERON:0002463) but this is outside the scope of this NTR.
